lede: A look at July 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Johnny Brackins and Andrés Madrid, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2024 Southport stabbings
events.desc: Three children are stabbed to death and 10 other people injured at a dance studio in Southport, England. This incident, coupled with widespread online misinformation, leads to various racially motivated riots across the UK.

events.title: Wedding of Prince Charles and Lady Diana Spencer
events.desc: A worldwide television audience of around 750 million people watch the wedding of Charles, Prince of Wales, and Lady Diana Spencer at St Paul's Cathedral in London.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: After four days, the No Gun Ri Massacre ends when the US Army 7th Cavalry Regiment is withdrawn.

events.title: Olympic Games
events.desc: Olympic Games: The Games of the XIV Olympiad: After a hiatus of 12 years caused by World War II, the first Summer Olympics to be held since the 1936 Summer Olympics in Berlin, open in London.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler becomes leader of the National Socialist German Workers' Party.

events.title: Augustin-Jean Fresnel
events.desc: French physicist Augustin Fresnel submits his prizewinning "Memoir on the Diffraction of Light", precisely accounting for the limited extent to which light spreads into shadows, and thereby demolishing the oldest objection to the wave theory of light.

events.title: Nine Years' War
events.desc: War of the Grand Alliance: Battle of Landen: France wins a victory over Allied forces in the Netherlands.

events.title: James VI and I
events.desc: The infant James VI is crowned King of Scotland at Stirling.

events.title: Byzantine–Bulgarian wars
events.desc: Byzantine–Bulgarian wars: Battle of Kleidion: Byzantine emperor Basil II inflicts a decisive defeat on the Bulgarian army, and his subsequent treatment of 15,000 prisoners reportedly causes Tsar Samuil of Bulgaria to die of a heart attack less than three months later, on October 6.

births.name: Luis Alicea
births.line: Puerto Rican-American baseball player and coach

births.name: Robert Kilpatrick, Baron Kilpatrick of Kincraig

deaths.text: Alon Abutbul, Israeli film, television and theater actor
Nikolai Krylenko, Russian lawyer, jurist, and politician, Prosecutor General of the Russian SFSR

observances.text: Christian feast day: Martha of Bethany (Catholic, Anglican, and Lutheran Church)
Christian feast day: Olaf II of Norway
Christian feast day: Simplicius, Faustinus and Beatrix
Earliest day on which Somer's Day can fall, while August 4 is the latest; celebrated on Friday before the first Monday in August. (Bermuda)
